question: After finding a candidate provider in Scout's partner directory, how do I inspect its full published profile without inventing capabilities?

golden.answer: Take the exact slug returned by partner discovery and call getPartner. Report only fields in the published profile—such as services, sectors, regions, contact or documentation links, verification, and freshness—and preserve the profile URL. Discovery selects candidates; detail verifies one candidate's public profile.
keyFacts: Calls getPartner with an exact slug obtained from partner discovery.
Reports capabilities and freshness only when present in the returned profile.
avoid: Do NOT fabricate a service, corridor, contact method, or compliance claim absent from the profile.
Do NOT use a display name where the operation requires a slug.
golden.notes: Live behavioral partner-detail case; no provider roster is pinned.

sources.note: Fetch-verified local path exists and defines the exercised contract/workflow.

evidence: 2026-07-11 localhost:8787 search→execute probe observed: HTTP 200; getPartners supplied slug trustless-work and getPartner returned its published detail envelope.
catalog/manifest.json exists and supports the behavioral golden.
Sibling sweep: reviewed existing battery cases sharing the exercised surface; no contradictory behavioral golden found.
